Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex Operation Dream Job, an expert-level threat actor, aims to disrupt transportation systems by gathering intel, establishing command and control, gaining initial access via spear-phishing, executing malicious payloads, maintaining persistence, escalating privileges, evading defenses, accessing credentials, discovering network details, moving laterally, collecting sensitive data, exfiltrating it, and ultimately causing service disruptions.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Vulnerability, etc.
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Vulnerability exploit, etc.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The threat actor gathers information about transportation systems, employees, and infrastructure using open-source intelligence techniques.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 - Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The threat actor establishes command and control servers in the public and private clouds to support the operation.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-3118 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor targets key personnel in transportation systems with tailored phishing emails containing malicious attachments exploiting the Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerability.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dream Job employs a complex attack strategy targeting transportation systems, involving multiple stages from reconnaissance to impact, utilizing various techniques and exploiting known vulnerabilities along the way.
